title: cactus microcephalus
intro: Cactus microcephalus, also known as the little head cactus or pitaya, is a species of cactus that belongs to the genus Cereus. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating wounds, and providing relief from respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, Cactus microcephalus is often used in teas, infusions, and creams to harness its medicinal properties.
